free_text: Inelastic scattering in the 6Li + 9Be system leading
to unbound excited states of either nucleus
University of the Witwatersrand,
Johannesburg, 2050 South Africa
EN Tandem Van de Graaff accelerator
of the University of the Witwatersran
gas-ionization
chamber description
The self-supporting 9Be targets had an areal density
of ~40 mu-g/cm2. In the case of 6Li, targets
consisted a backing layer of carbon (~10 mu-g/cm2)
on which ~50 mu-g/cm2 of 99.9% isotopically pure 6Li
was deposited by vacuum evaporation. The 6Li target
also contained 197Au of ~5 mu-g/cm2, which led to
Rutherford scattering by the 9Be nuclei and provided
a reference point for energy calibration in the
measured energy spectra.
The Li and Be beams produced by an IONEX 860A sputter
source. The beam current on the the target was between
50 and 60 nA of 6Li3+ and 5 to 8 nAof 9Be3+.
Detection and identification of the reaction
products was accomplished with a dE-E gas-ionization
chamber which has been described previously in detail:
B.0.Carragher,F Nucl.Phys. A460 (1986) 341
A solid-state detector at an angle of 45.degr.
served as a monitor for normalization between runs.
Digitizing error
